D1 Optics L2 Understand Reference

Umbra and Penumbra

widen the source and watch a hard shadow grow a soft penumbral fringe

An extended source casts two shadow regions — a fully dark umbra where the source is hidden entirely, and a soft penumbra where part of the source is visible.

Slide the source radius S upward. The umbra shrinks; if the source becomes larger than the object, the umbra vanishes — only a penumbra remains, as during an annular eclipse.
